title: A FAFB left-right brain registration
text: Build left-right (L-R) registration fly brain: diffeomorphism maps one hemisphere onto mirror image , structures left can compared directly counterparts right. package-native, Deformetrica-4 re-implementation FAFB L-R bridging registration flyconnectome/deformetricaLR. register whole FAFB brain surface mirror image — flipped brain (red) flows onto original (grey) fitted diffeomorphism, correcting brain’s L-R asymmetry:
code: library(deformetricar) library(nat) library(elmr)     # elmr::FAFB.surf, the FAFB brain surface library(Rvcg)     # mesh decimation

what: 1. The FAFB brain and its mirror
title: A FAFB left-right brain registration
code: brain <- as.mesh3d(elmr::FAFB.surf)          # FAFB whole-brain surface xyzmatrix(brain) <- xyzmatrix(brain) / 1000  # nm -> um: Deformetrica needs ~O(1-100) coords  ## Reflect across the brain's X-midline; flip face winding so normals stay outward ## (SurfaceMesh / Current attachment uses them). V <- t(brain$vb[1:3, ]); midX <- mean(range(V[, 1])) mir <- brain mir$vb[1, ] <- 2 * midX - brain$vb[1, ] mir$it <- mir$it[c(2, 1, 3), ]

title: A FAFB left-right brain registration
text: Decimate surfaces, fit one diffeomorphism mapping flipped brain onto original (SurfaceMesh + Current — vertex correspondence needed). kernel width set bounding box control-point grid stays bounded.
code: src <- Rvcg::vcgQEdecim(mir,   tarface = 4000)   # flipped (moving) tgt <- Rvcg::vcgQEdecim(brain, tarface = 4000)   # original (fixed) kw  <- sqrt(sum((apply(V, 2, max) - apply(V, 2, min))^2)) / 15  fit <- deformetrica_register(src, tgt, kernel_width = kw, timepoints = 15,                              max_iterations = 100, device = "auto", verbose = TRUE)

what: 3. Apply + GIF of the L-R flow
title: A FAFB left-right brain registration
text: deformetrica_shoot() flows object fitted diffeomorphism (returning class give ). flow = TRUE returns every geodesic timepoint, ggplot_flow_gif() animates nat.ggplot: flipped brain (red) flowing onto translucent original.
code: flow <- deformetrica_shoot(mir, fit$control_points, fit$momenta,                            kernel_width = fit$kernel_width, flow = TRUE)  # list of meshes  ggplot_flow_gif(list(mirror = flow), cols = c(mirror = "#EE4266"), alpha = 0.45,                 volume = brain, volume_col = "#2C7FB8", volume_alpha = 0.09,                 rotation_matrix = diag(c(1, -1, 1, 1)),   # dorsal-up frontal view                 file = "fafb_left_right.gif")   # needs gifski or magick

what: 4. Validation — does the warp tighten the L-R match?
title: A FAFB left-right brain registration
text: registration good warped mirror brain sits closer original raw flip . Score symmetric surface distance.
code: final    <- deformetrica_shoot(mir, fit$control_points, fit$momenta, kernel_width = fit$kernel_width) d_flip   <- Rvcg::vcgClostKD(nat::xyzmatrix(mir),   brain)$quality d_warped <- Rvcg::vcgClostKD(nat::xyzmatrix(final), brain)$quality c(flip = median(abs(d_flip)), warped = median(abs(d_warped)))   # warped should be smaller

title: A FAFB left-right brain registration
text: deformetricaLR built L-R registration brain surface set matched cognate neuron tracts — one diffeomorphism fit many objects . deformetrica_register_multi() exactly , mixes object types (meshes, neurons polylines, point sets):
code: # sources / targets: named lists of matched objects (e.g. left/right cognate neurons) fit <- deformetrica_register_multi(   sources = list(DA1 = right_DA1, MB = right_MB),   targets = list(DA1 = left_DA1,  MB = left_MB),   kernel_width = 15,   landmarks = list(source = lm_right, target = lm_left))   # optional global anchor

title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: L1 larval connectome — first-instar Drosophila larval CNS, manually reconstructed hosted openly Virtual Fly Brain — nearly bilaterally symmetric. vignette builds left-right symmetrising diffeomorphism whole L1 CNS (brain ventral nerve cord) matched left/right neuropil compartments, uses carry structures across midline onto contralateral partners, finds cognate neuron pairs morphological similarity (NBLAST) two sides register. Ten years ago package shipped three hard-wired, L1-helpers — apply.mirror.affine(), symmetrisel1() otherside() — backed bundled transform. deformetricar longer L1-specific, functions gone package. vignette rebuilds scratch, lines general deformetricar API, driven live VFB dataset. point walkthrough: three functions deliverable.
code: library(deformetricar) library(nat) library(catmaid)          # aka rcatmaid library(Rvcg)             # mesh clean / decimate library(Morpho)           # applyTransform

what: 1. Fetch the L1 CNS and its left/right compartments (CATMAID)
title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: L1 larval CATMAID public read-. ships whole-CNS surface (cns) plus set bilateral neuropil volumes — two brain hemispheres, SEZ, thoracic/abdominal VNC segments (T1–T3, A1–A8) — _left/_right pair. matched pairs anchors teach warp “symmetric” means; take spread whole length CNS.
code: l1 <- catmaid_connection(server = "https://l1em.catmaid.virtualflybrain.org/") vl <- catmaid_get_volumelist(conn = l1) # Deformetrica needs ~O(1-100) coordinates: work in um, not the raw nm. nm2um  <- function(x) { xyzmatrix(x) <- xyzmatrix(x) / 1000; x } getvol <- function(name) nm2um(Rvcg::vcgClean(   as.mesh3d(catmaid_get_volume(vl$id[vl$name == name], conn = l1, rval = "mesh3d")),   sel = 0, silent = TRUE))  cns   <- getvol("cns")                                    # the whole CNS surface pairs <- c("Brain Hemisphere", "SEZ", "T1", "T2", "A3", "A6")   # head -> tail spread lname <- function(p) if (p == "Brain Hemisphere") "Brain Hemisphere left"  else paste0(p, "_left") rname <- function(p) if (p == "Brain Hemisphere") "Brain Hemisphere right" else paste0(p, "_right") L <- setNames(lapply(pairs, function(p) getvol(lname(p))), pairs) R <- setNames(lapply(pairs, function(p) getvol(rname(p))), pairs)

what: 2. apply.mirror.affine() — reflect one side onto the other
title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: linear half symmetrising map. Reflect object across CNS midline plane (left ↔︎ right), rigidly affine-align reflection back onto original CNS — removes fact specimen’s midline neither exactly x = 0 exactly axis-aligned. Everything downstream rides transform, fit (whole CNS) wrap function.
code: midX   <- mean(range(xyzmatrix(cns)[, 1]))                # specimen midline (x) mirror <- function(m) {                                   # reflect across x = midX   xyzmatrix(m)[, 1] <- 2 * midX - xyzmatrix(m)[, 1]   if (!is.null(m$it)) m$it <- m$it[c(2, 1, 3), ]          # keep surface winding outward   m$normals <- NULL; m } pre <- affine_prealign(mirror(cns), cns, type = "rigid")  # reflection -> original CNS  # apply.mirror.affine(x): reflect x, then apply that same rigid alignment. apply.mirror.affine <- function(x) pre$apply(mirror(x))

what: 3. Fit the symmetrising diffeomorphism from the matched compartments
title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: affine capture specimen’s non-rigid left-right asymmetry (slightly bent VNC, one hemisphere fuller ). diffeomorphism can. fit one diffeomorphism whole set matched compartments deformetrica_register_multi(), directions result genuinely symmetric: mirror-affined right compartment driven onto left partner, mirror-affined left onto right. Registering compartment surfaces (~thousands neurons) keeps cheap robust.
code: dec  <- function(m, n = 700L) Rvcg::vcgQEdecim(m, tarface = n) srcs <- tgts <- list() for (p in pairs) {   srcs[[paste0(p, "_R")]] <- dec(apply.mirror.affine(R[[p]])); tgts[[paste0(p, "_R")]] <- dec(L[[p]])   srcs[[paste0(p, "_L")]] <- dec(apply.mirror.affine(L[[p]])); tgts[[paste0(p, "_L")]] <- dec(R[[p]]) } kw  <- sqrt(sum((apply(xyzmatrix(cns), 2, max) - apply(xyzmatrix(cns), 2, min))^2)) / 12 fit <- deformetrica_register_multi(srcs, tgts, kernel_width = kw, data_sigma = 3,                                    timepoints = 15L, max_iterations = 40L, device = "auto")

what: 4. symmetrisel1() and otherside() — the full contralateral map
title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: Now compose two halves. symmetrisel1() mirror-affines object flows fitted diffeomorphism — complete non-linear symmetrisation. diffeomorphism makes CNS symmetric exactly map carries structure onto contralateral partner, otherside() — “send neuron side CNS” — function. (flow = TRUE keeps every geodesic timepoint, animation; default returns just endpoint.) three functions — apply.mirror.affine(), symmetrisel1(), otherside() — modern, dataset-driven replacements old bundled helpers. accept anything xyzmatrix() coordinates: points matrix, mesh3d, neuron neuronlist.
code: symmetrisel1 <- function(x, flow = FALSE)   deformetrica_shoot(apply.mirror.affine(x), fit$control_points, fit$momenta,                      kernel_width = fit$kernel_width, flow = flow)  otherside <- symmetrisel1        # the symmetrising map IS the left<->right map

what: 5. Watch the map carry the right cells onto their left cognates
title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: whole L1 CNS nearly symmetric surface deformation µm — true biology, subtle watch. striking thing see map applied cells: send right Kenyon cells across midline otherside() watch land left partners. animate full journey two acts — mirror-affine reflection (big, ~50 µm cross-midline sweep) diffeomorphic flow — static grey CNS, viewed along long axis (first principal component, head--tail) bilateral symmetry edge-. left Kenyon cells (blue) drawn static target right ones (rose) meet.
code: # One Kenyon-cell fetcher for the whole vignette: n = NULL keeps them all (§6), # n = 36 takes a legible subset for the animation. getkc <- function(ann, n = NULL) {   x <- read.neurons.catmaid(catmaid_skids(ann, conn = l1), conn = l1)   x <- nat::nlapply(nat::nlapply(x, nat::resample, stepsize = 1500), nm2um)   if (!is.null(n) && length(x) > n) x[round(seq(1, length(x), length.out = n))] else x } kcR <- getkc("annotation:Kenyon Cell right", n = 36) kcL <- getkc("annotation:Kenyon Cell left",  n = 36)  # the two acts of otherside(): the mirror-affine sweep, then the diffeomorphic flow interp  <- function(o, from, to, n) lapply(seq(0, 1, length.out = n),              function(a) { x <- o; xyzmatrix(x) <- (1 - a) * from + a * to; x }) aff_kc  <- apply.mirror.affine(kcR)                                    # reflect + rigid act1    <- interp(kcR, xyzmatrix(kcR), xyzmatrix(aff_kc), 12)          # the cross-midline sweep act2    <- deformetrica_shoot(aff_kc, fit$control_points, fit$momenta,                               kernel_width = fit$kernel_width, flow = TRUE)   # the diffeomorphism flow_kc <- c(act1, act2)  # each Kenyon cell is rooted at its soma, so its root point IS the cell body: draw # those as circles (the moving right somata, the static left ones) with `points`. soma_of <- function(nl) do.call(rbind, lapply(nl, function(n) xyzmatrix(n)[nat::rootpoints(n)[1], , drop = FALSE])) somata_R <- lapply(flow_kc, soma_of)          # per-frame, move with the sweep somata_L <- soma_of(kcL)                       # static targets  # view: long axis (PC1) vertical, next axis horizontal, shortest into the screen pc <- prcomp(xyzmatrix(cns))$rotation RM <- diag(4); RM[1, 1:3] <- pc[, 2]; RM[2, 1:3] <- -pc[, 1]; RM[3, 1:3] <- pc[, 3] ggplot_flow_gif(list(KC_right = flow_kc),                 cols = list(KC_right = "#FF5C8A"), alpha = c(KC_right = 0.35),                 targets = list(KC_left = kcL), target_cols = c(KC_left = "#2C7FB8"),                 target_alpha = 0.28,                 points = list(KC_right = somata_R, KC_left = somata_L),                 point_cols = c(KC_right = "#FF5C8A", KC_left = "#2C7FB8"),                 point_size = 3, point_alpha = 0.85,                 volume = cns, volume_col = "grey65", volume_alpha = 0.10,                 rotation_matrix = RM, delay = 0.12,                 file = "l1_symmetrise.gif")   # somata as circles; grey CNS + left KCs as context

title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: symmetrising map hand, cognate-finding direct: send every right neuron side otherside(), NBLAST left neurons — top hit putative left cognate. Kenyon cells clean test case: exist matched numbers sides, rights map cleanly onto left. Compare nblast(dp(apply.mirror.affine(KCR)), dp(KCL)) (mirror-affine ) symmetrised scores: diffeomorphism raise correct-cognate NBLAST sharpen top-1 margin, making hemilineage- cell-type-level left-right mapping clearer.
code: library(nat.nblast) # the FULL Kenyon-cell sets (the animation above used a legible subset) KCR <- getkc("annotation:Kenyon Cell right") KCL <- getkc("annotation:Kenyon Cell left")  kcR_other <- otherside(KCR)                              # every right KC, warped onto the left dp <- function(nl) nat::dotprops(nl, resample = 1, k = 5, .progress = "none") scores  <- nat.nblast::nblast(dp(kcR_other), dp(KCL), normalised = TRUE) cognate <- apply(scores, 2, function(s) rownames(scores)[which.max(s)]) head(data.frame(left = colnames(scores), right_cognate = cognate))

title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: compartments, neurons, drive fit. Registering handful decimated compartment surfaces cheap numerically stable; fitting thousands neuron skeletons neither. neurons apply finished map — always cheap (deformetrica_shoot()). three functions point. apply.mirror.affine(), symmetrisel1() otherside() just closures pre fit. Swap dataset (compartment list) symmetriser nervous system — nothing L1-specific , exactly longer live package. anchors, better VNC. Add A1–A8 / T1–T3 pairs pairs tighter fit along nerve cord, cost slower registration.

title: Symmetrising the L1 larval CNS and finding left-right cognates
text: whole-brain neuropil surface analogues workflow mosquito--fly FAFB left-right vignettes.

title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: Take cell type reconstructed two different adult Drosophila connectomes — isolated-CNS EM volume maleCNS whole central-nervous-system BANC — warp one onto neurons can compared single coordinate frame. use DA1 antennal-lobe projection neurons (DA1_lPN + DA1_vPN), small, well-defined, bilaterally paired population. registration driven neurons’ shape, matched per cell type side, anchored cell bodies: template bridge maleCNS → FAFB14 → JRC2018F → BANC lands maleCNS neurons top BANC ones within microns; neuron rooted soma reduced simplified core frame (nat::simplify_neuron()), surface mesh decimated matched complexity sides; multi-object diffeomorphism (deformetrica_register_multi()) fits, per (cell type, side), core-frame skeletons (Varifold, weighted strongly) neuron-surface meshes (Current), plus ordered soma / root-point Landmark correspondence weak whole-brain hull anchor; nat.ggplot animation (ggplot_flow_gif()) geodesic flow, somata shown circles. Everything runs locally CPU (device = "cpu"); device = "auto" uses CUDA GPU present. Work microns — Deformetrica needs O(1–100) coordinates, fit nm can silently collapse identity warp. BANC maleCNS data come nm, divide 1000 throughout.
code: library(deformetricar) library(nat) library(malecns)        # maleCNS (male-cns:v0.9) neuprint client + bridging landmarks library(bancr)          # BANC CAVE/neuprint client + JRC2018F<->BANC bridge + surfaces library(neuprintr) library(nat.flybrains); library(nat.jrcbrains); library(nat.templatebrains) library(fafbseg) library(Rvcg); library(Morpho)   # mesh decimation / merging  # Access. The BANC *metadata* used here needs no BANC login: banc_meta() and # banc_nuclei() default to the public GCS snapshot (source = "gcs"). Only the neuron # *geometry* — banc_read_l2skel()/banc_read_neuron_meshes() — needs a BANC CAVE token, # and the maleCNS neuPrint side needs a malecns token. See the bancr, malecns and # neuprintr READMEs for getting those into ~/.Renviron. # # One-time: set up the Deformetrica CLI this package wraps, in a managed conda env. # install_deformetrica()      # (skip if find_deformetrica() already resolves)

what: 1. Fetch both datasets: metadata, skeletons, meshes, somata
title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: connectomes annotate DA1 uniglomerular projection neurons DA1_lPN DA1_vPN (exclude receptor neurons ORN_DA1). keep neuron’s cell type side, drop neuron missing either — registration matches strictly (type, side). Neuron meshes large (hundreds thousands faces), decimate common target — key matched complexity sides Current attachment compares like like.
code: um <- function(x) { xyzmatrix(x) <- xyzmatrix(x) / 1000; x }  ## --- maleCNS: metadata, skeletons, somata (all native nm) --- mm <- mcns_neuprint_meta("DA1") mm <- mm[mm$type %in% c("DA1_lPN", "DA1_vPN") & mm$somaSide %in% c("L", "R"), ] mn <- nlapply(read_mcns_neurons(mm$bodyid), resample, stepsize = 1000) # somaLocation is an "x,y,z" string in 8 nm voxels -> native nm: msoma <- do.call(rbind, lapply(strsplit(mm$somaLocation, ","), as.numeric)) * 8  ## --- BANC: metadata, L2 skeletons, somata from the nucleus table (native nm) --- bm <- banc_meta() bm <- bm[bm$type %in% c("DA1_lPN", "DA1_vPN") & bm$side %in% c("left", "right"), ] bn <- nlapply(banc_read_l2skel(bm$id), resample, stepsize = 1000) nuc   <- banc_nuclei(rootids = bm$id)                 # nucleus_position_nm (nm) bsoma <- data.frame(xyzmatrix(nuc$nucleus_position_nm),                     root_id = as.character(nuc$root_id)) TARFACE <- 1000L mme <- nlapply(read_mcns_meshes(mm$bodyid, units = "nm"), Rvcg::vcgQEdecim, tarface = TARFACE) bme <- nlapply(banc_read_neuron_meshes(bm$id),            Rvcg::vcgQEdecim, tarface = TARFACE)

what: 2. Bridge the maleCNS side into BANC space
title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: maleCNS BANC template. hop shared fly templates — malecns package ships landmark bridges FAFB14, nat.jrcbrains carries FAFB14 → JRC2018F, bancr inverts JRC2018F ↔︎ BANC thin-plate spline — carrying neurons, meshes somata transform.
code: malecns:::mcns_register_xforms()      # register the bundled malecns<->FAFB14 bridges to_banc <- function(x) {   x <- xform_brain(x, sample = "malecns", reference = "FAFB14")   x <- xform_brain(x, sample = "FAFB14",  reference = "JRC2018F")   banc_to_JRC2018F(x, region = "brain", banc.units = "nm", inverse = TRUE)  # -> BANC nm } mn  <- um(to_banc(mn));                             bn  <- um(bn) mme <- nlapply(mme, function(m) um(`xyzmatrix<-`(m, value = to_banc(xyzmatrix(m))))) bme <- nlapply(bme, um) msoma <- to_banc(msoma) / 1000                      # BANC µm bsoma[c("X","Y","Z")] <- bsoma[c("X","Y","Z")] / 1000

title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: Root soma, simplify core frame. soma sits end backbone, first re-root neuron (nat::reroot() soma point), reduce main branches nat::simplify_neuron(). core frame aligns neuron’s skeleton without noise fine twigs (much lighter fit). Soma correspondence. Collapse dataset’s cell bodies centroid per (side, type) match groups two datasets share — ordered Landmark object pinning lateral (lPN) ventral (vPN) cell-body clusters.
code: msl <- setNames(asplit(msoma, 1), as.character(mm$bodyid)) bsl <- setNames(asplit(as.matrix(bsoma[c("X","Y","Z")]), 1), bsoma$root_id) core <- function(nl, somalist, n = 5) {   ids <- names(nl)   out <- lapply(seq_along(nl), function(i) {     x <- nl[[i]]; p <- somalist[[ ids[i] ]]     if (!is.null(p)) x <- tryCatch(reroot(x, point = as.numeric(p)), error = function(e) x)     resample(tryCatch(simplify_neuron(x, n = n), error = function(e) x), stepsize = 3)   })   as.neuronlist(setNames(out, ids)) } sdf <- function(xyz, side, type) data.frame(xyz, grp = paste(side, type)) mS  <- sdf(msoma, mm$somaSide, mm$type) bmap <- bm[match(bsoma$root_id, as.character(bm$id)), c("side", "type")] bS  <- sdf(as.matrix(bsoma[c("X","Y","Z")]),            ifelse(bmap$side == "left", "L", "R"), bmap$type) bS  <- bS[!is.na(bS$grp) & !is.na(bS[[1]]), ] cen <- function(df) do.call(rbind, lapply(split(df, df$grp),   function(s) data.frame(grp = s$grp[1], t(colMeans(as.matrix(s[1:3])))))) mc <- cen(mS); bc <- cen(bS); common <- intersect(mc$grp, bc$grp) soma_src <- as.matrix(mc[match(common, mc$grp), 2:4]) soma_tgt <- as.matrix(bc[match(common, bc$grp), 2:4]) rownames(soma_src) <- rownames(soma_tgt) <- common  # Weak identity anchor: sparse hull points pin the far field near-identity. hull <- um(as.mesh3d(bancr::banc_brain_neuropil.surf)) set.seed(1); anc <- xyzmatrix(hull)[sample(ncol(hull$vb), 400L), ]

what: 4. One diffeomorphism, matched per cell type and side
title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: deformetrica_register_multi() fits single diffeomorphism every object . (type, side) present datasets add core-frame skeleton (Varifold, weighted strongly via small data_sigma) merged, re-decimated surface mesh (Current). soma landmarks pin cell bodies; hull anchor keeps warp local.
code: mtype <- setNames(as.character(mm$type),  as.character(mm$bodyid))[names(mn)] btype <- setNames(as.character(bm$type),  as.character(bm$id))[names(bn)] msd   <- setNames(as.character(mm$somaSide), as.character(mm$bodyid))[names(mn)] bsd   <- setNames(ifelse(bm$side == "left", "L", "R"), as.character(bm$id))[names(bn)] mergedec <- function(nl) Rvcg::vcgQEdecim(   Rvcg::vcgClean(Morpho::mergeMeshes(unname(as.list(nl))), sel = 0:1), tarface = 1500L)  srcs <- tgts <- list(); okw <- sig <- c() for (s in c("L", "R")) for (g in c("DA1_lPN", "DA1_vPN")) {   mi <- names(mn)[msd == s & mtype == g]; bi <- names(bn)[bsd == s & btype == g]   if (!length(mi) || !length(bi)) next   k <- sprintf("PN_%s_%s", s, sub("DA1_", "", g))   srcs[[k]] <- core(mn[mi], msl); tgts[[k]] <- core(bn[bi], bsl); okw[k] <- 15; sig[k] <- 1   mk <- paste0(k, "_mesh")   srcs[[mk]] <- mergedec(mme[mi]); tgts[[mk]] <- mergedec(bme[bi]); okw[mk] <- 20; sig[mk] <- 2 } srcs$soma <- soma_src; tgts$soma <- soma_tgt; okw["soma"] <- 60; sig["soma"] <- 0.3  kw <- sqrt(sum((apply(xyzmatrix(hull), 2, max) - apply(xyzmatrix(hull), 2, min))^2)) / 15 fit <- deformetrica_register_multi(   srcs, tgts, kernel_width = kw, object_kernel_width = okw, data_sigma = sig,   landmarks = list(source = anc, target = anc), landmark_sigma = 8,   timepoints = 15L, max_iterations = 40L, device = "cpu")

what: 5. Animate the warp, with root points as circles
title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: deformetrica_shoot(..., flow = TRUE) returns every timepoint geodesic flow. ggplot_flow_gif() renders maleCNS neurons (red) flowing onto static BANC targets (blue) brain hull; points draws somata circles — moving maleCNS root points static BANC ones — cell-body match visible.
code: sh <- function(x) deformetrica_shoot(x, fit$control_points, fit$momenta,                                      kernel_width = fit$kernel_width, timepoints = 15L,                                      device = "cpu", flow = TRUE) mn_L <- mn[msd == "L"]; mn_R <- mn[msd == "R"] bn_L <- bn[bsd == "L"]; bn_R <- bn[bsd == "R"]  ggplot_flow_gif(   list(maleCNS_L = sh(mn_L), maleCNS_R = sh(mn_R)),   cols = c(maleCNS_L = "#EE4266", maleCNS_R = "#EE4266"), alpha = 0.9,   volume = Rvcg::vcgQEdecim(hull, tarface = 3000L), volume_col = "grey70", volume_alpha = 0.06,   targets = list(BANC_L = bn_L, BANC_R = bn_R),   target_cols = c(BANC_L = "#2C7FB8", BANC_R = "#2C7FB8"), target_alpha = 0.5,   points = list(maleCNS_soma = sh(msoma), BANC_soma = as.matrix(bsoma[c("X","Y","Z")])),   point_cols = c(maleCNS_soma = "#EE4266", BANC_soma = "#2C7FB8"), point_size = 3.2,   rotation_matrix = bancr::banc_rotation_matrices[["front"]], delay = 0.15,   file = "malecns_to_banc.gif")   # needs the gifski (or magick) package

title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: Score arbours median nearest-point distance warped maleCNS neuron nearest BANC neuron (per side), somata per-group centroid distance — beat template-bridge baseline. Typical numbers : arbours tighten ~1.0–1.1 µm (bridge) ~0.75–0.82 µm (warp), soma clusters mean ~26 µm ~13–23 µm.
code: nnd  <- function(a, b) median(Rvcg::vcgKDtree(xyzmatrix(b), xyzmatrix(a), k = 1)$distance) warp <- function(x) deformetrica_shoot(x, fit$control_points, fit$momenta,                                        kernel_width = fit$kernel_width) data.frame(side = c("L", "R"),            bridge = c(nnd(mn_L, bn_L),       nnd(mn_R, bn_R)),            warp   = c(nnd(warp(mn_L), bn_L), nnd(warp(mn_R), bn_R)))  ws <- xyzmatrix(warp(soma_src)) data.frame(group = rownames(soma_src),            bridge = sqrt(rowSums((soma_src - soma_tgt)^2)),            warp   = sqrt(rowSums((ws       - soma_tgt)^2)))

title: Warping maleCNS DA1 projection neurons onto the BANC
text: Match cell type side. rare DA1_vPN (one per side) drift pooled lPN-dominated bundle, (type, side) object; neuron missing either label dropped. Skeleton core frame + surface mesh. Rooting soma simplifying keeps neuron’s “core frame” tight Varifold fit; decimated mesh adds surface “mask” Current. Keep mesh complexity matched across datasets (decimated face count) surfaces comparable — meshes heavy, decimate harder Rvcg. Somata need landmark. soma one point negligible curve/surface mass, shape terms barely feel ; ordered soma Landmark pins cell bodies. lPN clusters align tightly; vPN somata sit ~30–50 µm apart even arbours match — genuine cross-dataset difference smooth warp fully absorb without tearing neuron. Use curated soma fields (maleCNS somaLocation, BANC banc_nuclei()), skeleton root, L2 skeleton need sit soma. Tuning levers. data_sigma (smaller = stronger), object_kernel_width (matching scale) global kernel_width (stiffness) trade global local fit; soma landmark’s weight main lever hard cell bodies pulled together. Template bridge. maleCNS → FAFB14 → JRC2018F → BANC hop reuses published registrations; swap final banc_to_JRC2018F() step target different dataset. ```

title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: Register whole-brain surface mosquito Aedes aegypti onto Drosophila JRC2018F template — driven cross-identified neuropils two brains share, matched left--left right--right warp respects animals’ bilateral symmetry. Everything plain R built deformetricar helpers. recipe one good bridging registration uses: affine pre-alignment remove gross pose scale (affine_prealign()), multi-object diffeomorphism (deformetrica_register_multi()) fit whole set matched neuropils , weighted, plus weak outer hull, nat.ggplot animation resulting geodesic flow (ggplot_flow_gif()). Everything runs locally CPU — GPU required. device = "auto" uses CUDA GPU one present transparently falls back CPU (whole-brain fit takes minutes CPU, longer GPU perfectly usable).
code: library(deformetricar) library(nat) library(nat.flybrains)   # JRC2018F template + JFRC2NP neuropils library(nat.jrcbrains)   # JFRC2 <-> JRC2018F bridging registration (for xform_brain below) library(Rvcg)            # mesh decimation # remotes::install_github("natverse/insectbrainr") library(insectbrainr)    # Aedes brain from the Insect Brain Database  # One-time: set up the Deformetrica CLI this package wraps, in a managed conda env. # (Skip if find_deformetrica() already resolves, e.g. options(deformetricar.exe=).) # install_deformetrica()  # One-time: the JFRC2 -> JRC2018F step below needs the Saalfeld-lab bridging # registrations (a few hundred MB). Loading nat.jrcbrains registers them; download # them once with: # nat.jrcbrains::download_saalfeldlab_registrations(filenames = "JRC2018F_JFRC2013.h5")

what: 1. The two brains and their neuropils
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: fly side JRC2018F outer surface plus standard neuropils, brought JRC2018F frame . mosquito side Aedes aegypti standard brain (hxsurf named neuropil sub-surfaces) — segmented atlas built Meg Younger’s lab browsable Mosquito Brain Browser; load Insect Brain Database mirror. Please credit source use Aedes brain.
code: fly     <- as.mesh3d(nat.flybrains::JRC2018F.surf) fly_np  <- nat.flybrains::JFRC2NP.surf xyzmatrix(fly_np) <- nat.templatebrains::xform_brain(   xyzmatrix(fly_np), sample = "JFRC2", reference = "JRC2018F")  # Aedes has a FEMALE-only reconstruction in the Insect Brain Database. aedes_brain <- insectbraindb_read_brain(species = "Aedes aegypti", brain.sex = "FEMALE")

title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: diffeomorphism absorb cross-species change pose scale — affine step’s job. affine_prealign() centres, isotropically scales rigidly refines mosquito onto fly, hands back apply() closure replays identical transform sub-object, every mosquito neuropil carried aligned frame consistently.
code: pre <- affine_prealign(as.mesh3d(aedes_brain), fly, type = "rigid") # Keep only each neuropil's largest connected component: some Insect Brain Database # surfaces carry a stray detached fragment that cannot warp onto the compact fly # target and would poke out of the brain. amesh <- function(region) pre$apply(Rvcg::vcgIsolated(as.mesh3d(subset(aedes_brain, region)))) fmesh <- function(region) as.mesh3d(subset(fly_np, region))

what: Drop the lamina from the deforming hull
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: animation shows whole aedes brain surface deforming onto fly. one mosquito neuropil fly central-brain counterpart : lamina (LA), first optic ganglion. left hull dragged onto empty fly space — visually wrong waste deformation. build deforming hull aedes brain minus lamina regions, LA-free hull carry every step . (affine map untouched, every neuropil position unchanged.)
code: lamina      <- grep("^LA(_|$)", aedes_brain$RegionList, value = TRUE)  # LA_LEFT, LA_RIGHT lamina      # the regions we exclude keep        <- setdiff(aedes_brain$RegionList, lamina) pre$aligned <- pre$apply(as.mesh3d(subset(aedes_brain, keep)))         # hull without lamina

title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: Homologous neuropils different names across taxa identity. match mosquito neuropil fly counterpart one object per side, fit can never swap left structure onto right one. lamina (LA) fly central-brain counterpart dropped.
code: # name = c(mosquito region regex, fly region); _L / _R kept as separate objects. paired <- list(   ME_L = c("^ME_LEFT","ME_L"),   ME_R = c("^ME_RIGHT","ME_R"),   LO_L = c("^LO_LEFT","LO_L"),   LO_R = c("^LO_RIGHT","LO_R"),   LOP_L= c("^LOP_LEFT","LOP_L"), LOP_R= c("^LOP_RIGHT","LOP_R"),   CA_L = c("^CA_LEFT","MB_CA_L"),CA_R = c("^CA_RIGHT","MB_CA_R"),   PED_L= c("^PED_LEFT","MB_PED_L"),PED_R=c("^PED_RIGHT","MB_PED_R"),   ML_L = c("^ML_LEFT","MB_ML_L"),ML_R = c("^ML_RIGHT","MB_ML_R"),   VL_L = c("^VL_LEFT","MB_VL_L"),VL_R = c("^VL_RIGHT","MB_VL_R"),   AL_L = c("^AL_LEFT","AL_L"),   AL_R = c("^AL_RIGHT","AL_R"),   AMMC_L=c("^AMMC_LEFT","AMMC_L"),AMMC_R=c("^AMMC_RIGHT","AMMC_R"),   LAL_L= c("^LAL_LEFT","LAL_L"), LAL_R= c("^LAL_RIGHT","LAL_R"),   GA_L = c("^GA_LEFT","GA_L"),   GA_R = c("^GA_RIGHT","GA_R"),   AOTU_L=c("^AOTU-UU_LEFT","AOTU_L"),AOTU_R=c("^AOTU-UU_RIGHT","AOTU_R")) first <- function(hx, pat) grep(pat, hx$RegionList, value = TRUE)[1] srcs <- tgts <- list() for (nm in names(paired)) {   srcs[[nm]] <- amesh(first(aedes_brain, paired[[nm]][1]))   tgts[[nm]] <- fmesh(paired[[nm]][2]) }

what: The central complex — a naming and a shape difference
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: Insect central-complex parts named differently mosquito fly, one different shape. mosquito central body upper / lower divisions (CBU / CBL) homologues fly fan-shaped body (FB) ellipsoid body (EB); mosquito protocerebral bridge (PB) noduli () match fly PB . Crucially fly EB closed ring, whereas mosquito CBL open arch — forcing whole shapes together distorts ring. instead split midline CX object left right halves (split_mesh_lr()) match per half, keeps deformation symmetric. Splitting unpaired object mini-registration. midline object left/right label, every point still belongs hemisphere. mirror_lr_split() recovers way mirroring registration (cf. bancr::banc_lr): reflect object, optionally warp onto reflection find true (curved) symmetry surface, label point far direction must travel meet mirror partner. split_mesh_lr() flat-plane special case.
code: mid_a <- mean(range(xyzmatrix(pre$aligned)[,1])) mid_f <- mean(range(xyzmatrix(fly)[,1])) cx <- list(FB = c("^FB/CBU","FB"), EB = c("^EB/CBL","EB"), PB = c("^PB_noside","PB")) for (nm in names(cx)) {   a <- split_mesh_lr(amesh(first(aedes_brain, cx[[nm]][1])), mid = mid_a)   f <- split_mesh_lr(fmesh(cx[[nm]][2]), mid = mid_f)   srcs[[paste0(nm,"_L")]] <- a$L; tgts[[paste0(nm,"_L")]] <- f$L   srcs[[paste0(nm,"_R")]] <- a$R; tgts[[paste0(nm,"_R")]] <- f$R } # Noduli: mosquito NO is already L/R; the fly's single NO we split by its midline. fno <- split_mesh_lr(fmesh("NO"), mid = mid_f) srcs$NO_L <- amesh("^NO_LEFT");  tgts$NO_L <- fno$L srcs$NO_R <- amesh("^NO_RIGHT"); tgts$NO_R <- fno$R

what: Refine the affine on neuropil centroids
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: whole-brain rigid affine step 2 aligns two hulls, mosquito’s internal neuropils still sit systematically ventral fly counterparts (~26 µm Y) — brains pack interiors differently. diffeomorphism can absorb , stiff, global kernel drag small structures (galls, noduli, ellipsoid body) part way. refine init second similarity affine fit matched neuropil centroids, removes gross offset lets warp local, gross, work. carry every source neuropil outer hull identical map whole template stays one frame.
code: ctr    <- function(m) colMeans(xyzmatrix(m)) M      <- Morpho::computeTransform(t(sapply(tgts, ctr)), t(sapply(srcs, ctr)),                                    type = "similarity") applyM <- function(m) { xyzmatrix(m) <- Morpho::applyTransform(xyzmatrix(m), M); m } srcs        <- lapply(srcs, applyM) pre$aligned <- applyM(pre$aligned)   # the outer hull rides the same refinement

what: 4. One diffeomorphism for the whole matched set
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: deformetrica_register_multi() fits single diffeomorphism every matched object . data_sigma weights object — smaller means stronger — let well-matched neuropils drive fit, damp ring-vs-arch central complex, add outer hull weak global guide.
code: srcs <- lapply(srcs, Rvcg::vcgQEdecim, tarface = 4000L) tgts <- lapply(tgts, Rvcg::vcgQEdecim, tarface = 4000L) srcs$outer <- Rvcg::vcgQEdecim(pre$aligned, tarface = 12000L) tgts$outer <- Rvcg::vcgQEdecim(fly,         tarface = 12000L)  # noise-std: SMALLER = stronger attachment. Neuropils strong; the outer hull is only # a weak global guide; and — now the centroid refinement has removed the gross offset # — the central complex is weighted PART BY PART to fix its specific shape mismatches: # the ellipsoid body strongest (to circularise the open aedes CBL onto the fly ring), # the fan-shaped and protocerebral bridges stronger (to match their curvature), and # the galls strongest of all (to snap onto the small fly GA). (Tuned by iterating.) sigma <- setNames(rep(3.0, length(srcs)), names(srcs))  # neuropils: strong sigma["outer"] <- 4.0                                   # hull: weak global envelope (contains the MB lobes) sigma[grep("^(VL|ML|PED)_", names(sigma))] <- 1.0       # MB lobes: strongest (compress the elongated vertical lobe) sigma[grep("^EB_", names(sigma))] <- 1.5                # ellipsoid body: circularise the open arch onto the ring sigma[grep("^(FB|PB)_", names(sigma))] <- 2.0           # fan-shaped body / protocerebral bridge: match curvature sigma[grep("^GA_", names(sigma))] <- 0.5                # galls: strongest of all, snap onto the small fly GA sigma[grep("^NO_", names(sigma))] <- 1.5                # noduli: land + resolve left/right  # Kernel width sets the deformation's spatial stiffness. Too large (~diagonal / 11) # and it is so global that small peripheral structures (galls, noduli) get dragged by # their big neighbours; a little smaller (~diagonal / 15) lets them deform locally onto # their targets while the elongated optic lobes and MB lobes still move coherently # (medulla surface error dropped from ~22 to ~4 um, noduli roughly halved). Set it from # the target bounding box. kw <- sqrt(sum((apply(xyzmatrix(fly), 2, max) - apply(xyzmatrix(fly), 2, min))^2)) / 15 fit <- deformetrica_register_multi(   srcs, tgts, kernel_width = kw, data_sigma = sigma,   timepoints = 15L, max_iterations = 60L, device = "auto")

what: 5. Animate the flow, coloured by homology
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: deformetrica_shoot(..., flow = TRUE) returns every timepoint geodesic flow; ggplot_flow_gif() renders nat.ggplot, neuropil colour translucent fly brain, assembles GIF. central-complex close-(mosquito_cx_to_fly.gif), animate just FB/EB/PB/(gall GA) objects merged fly central complex — fly CX neuropils merged one light-pink volume, individual fly CX target still drawn transparent greyscale underneath.
code: show  <- setdiff(names(srcs), "outer")            # every cross-identified neuropil # Shoot the neuropils AND the whole aedes brain hull, so the hull deforms too. flows <- lapply(srcs[c("outer", show)], deformetrica_shoot,                 control_points = fit$control_points, momenta = fit$momenta,                 kernel_width = fit$kernel_width, timepoints = 15L,                 device = "auto", flow = TRUE) # Colour by functional group (L/R matched), shaded within group, LaCroix-inspired: # optic = blue, mushroom body = pink-purple, central complex = yellow-orange, the SEZ # AMMC green, and the rest of the mid brain cerise. grp <- function(b) {   if (b %in% c("ME","LO","LOP")) "optic"   else if (b %in% c("AMMC")) "sez"   else if (b %in% c("CA","PED","VL","ML")) "mb"   else if (b %in% c("FB","EB","PB","NO")) "cx"   else "midbrain" } grp_ramp <- list(optic=c("#1BA3C6","#B3ECF7"), sez=c("#2CB11B","#CBEFA0"),                  mb=c("#C1478E","#EDBFE0"), cx=c("#F5850B","#FFD24D"),                  midbrain=c("#C1275E","#F2A6C2")) sb  <- sub("_[LR]$", "", show); groups <- vapply(sb, grp, character(1)) cols <- setNames(character(length(show)), show) for (g in names(grp_ramp)) { gb <- unique(sb[groups == g]); if (!length(gb)) next   ramp <- setNames(grDevices::colorRampPalette(grp_ramp[[g]])(length(gb)), gb)   cols[groups == g] <- ramp[sb[groups == g]] }  # Fly context for the GIF: the whole brain hull as a very translucent light-pink # envelope, and each matched fly *target* neuropil in a transparent greyscale, so the # coloured mosquito neuropils can be read landing on their fly counterparts. The # rotation flips Y to the canonical frontal view (dorsal up); the raw X-Y projection # is upside down. rmat <- diag(c(1, -1, 1, 1)) # The aedes hull is drawn first (behind), baby-pink + translucent so we watch it deform # behind the coloured neuropils; a slow delay keeps the whole GIF ~5 s so the warp reads. cols_all  <- c(list(outer = "#F7C6D0"), as.list(cols)) alpha_all <- c(outer = 0.08, setNames(rep(0.9, length(show)), show)) ggplot_flow_gif(flows[c("outer", show)], cols = cols_all, alpha = alpha_all,                 rotation_matrix = rmat, delay = 0.25,                 volume = fly, volume_col = "lightpink", volume_alpha = 0.08,                 targets = tgts[show], target_alpha = 0.18,                 file = "mosquito_to_fly.gif")   # needs the gifski (or magick) package cxn    <- grep("^(FB|EB|PB|NO|GA)_", show, value = TRUE) cxvol  <- Rvcg::vcgClean(Morpho::mergeMeshes(unname(tgts[cxn])), sel = 0)  # merged fly CX ggplot_flow_gif(flows[cxn], cols = cols[cxn], rotation_matrix = rmat,                 volume = cxvol, volume_col = "lightpink", volume_alpha = 0.06,                 targets = tgts[cxn], target_alpha = 0.22,                 file = "mosquito_cx_to_fly.gif")

what: 6. Validation — do the neuropils land on their counterparts?
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: meaningful test whether warped mosquito neuropil lands fly homologue. Warp report symmetric surface distance volumetric Dice overlap; warp beat affine-baseline every neuropil.
code: surface_dist <- function(a, b) mean(Rvcg::vcgClostKD(a, b)$quality) dice <- function(a, b) {   ov <- Rvcg::vcgBool(a, b, "intersection")   2 * Rvcg::vcgVolume(ov) / (Rvcg::vcgVolume(a) + Rvcg::vcgVolume(b)) } score <- function(nm) {   w <- deformetrica_shoot(srcs[[nm]], fit$control_points, fit$momenta,                           kernel_width = fit$kernel_width)   data.frame(neuropil = nm, affine = surface_dist(srcs[[nm]], tgts[[nm]]),              warp = surface_dist(w, tgts[[nm]]), dice = dice(w, tgts[[nm]])) } do.call(rbind, lapply(names(srcs), score))

what: 7. Package the whole bridge as one reusable transform (TPS)
title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: Every aedes point interest now carried fly template — affine pre-alignment, centroid refinement, Deformetrica warp. can fold one thin-plate-spline transform ([Morpho::tps3d()]), fit dense sample native aedes positions lands fly space, reuse bridge new aedes object fly template single call — Deformetrica needed apply time. TPS needs start/end correspondences, earlier steps free elaborate like — example separate affine per neuropil (aligned fly counterpart) plus hull, rather one global affine, can tighten structures like mushroom-body lobes. However internal warp built, final start -> end TPS still captures whole thing single, portable bridge.
code: # a dense sample of NATIVE aedes positions (the whole-brain surface vertices) ... brain_native <- as.mesh3d(aedes_brain) start <- xyzmatrix(brain_native) # ... and where each lands after the FULL pipeline (affine -> centroid refine -> warp): carried <- applyM(pre$apply(brain_native))                       # affine + centroid refine end     <- xyzmatrix(deformetrica_shoot(carried, fit$control_points, fit$momenta,                                         kernel_width = fit$kernel_width))  # One reusable mosquito -> fly bridge (affine AND warp), as a thin-plate spline: mosquito_to_fly <- function(x) { xyzmatrix(x) <- Morpho::tps3d(xyzmatrix(x), start, end); x } # e.g. bridge a fresh aedes neuron / mesh into JRC2018F: # fly_space_obj <- mosquito_to_fly(some_native_aedes_object)

title: Warping a mosquito brain onto the fly by cross-identified neuropils
text: Central-complex nomenclature (CBU/CBL vs FB/EB, ring-vs-arch shape lower unit) follows comparative insect central-complex literature; see fly reference atlas (Hulse et al., eLife 2021) fly definitions. lamina excluded throughout — matched neuropils deforming whole-brain hull — fly central-brain counterpart. Aedes brain credit. Aedes aegypti standard brain segmented atlas Meg Younger’s lab (Mosquito Brain Browser), loaded via Insect Brain Database. Please cite source. Tuning levers. Three parameters trade global local fit, per object: data_sigma (attachment weight, smaller = stronger), object_kernel_width (scale surface mismatch measured, smaller = finer) global kernel_width (deformation stiffness). whole-brain error ~3–4 µm almost every neuropil; smaller kernel_width (~diagonal/15) needed small peripheral structures dragged large neighbours. galls (GA) known hard case: tiny far-lateral, single global diffeomorphism drags fly target matter weight, kernel width object kernel. structure like , register deformetrica_register() rather whole-brain fit. Replace whole-brain fit single homologous pair (e.g. mosquito AL → fly AL) via deformetrica_register() tighter, local registration.

what: What Deformetrica 4.x changed (vs the 2.1 API this package was written for)
title: deformetricar modernisation (branch dev)
text: ShootAndFlow3 / sparseMatchings3 C++ binaries. single deformetrica CLI subcommands estimate compute. Shooting deformetrica compute + <model-type>Shooting model.xml referencing <initial-control-points> <initial-momenta> (estimator’s ..._EstimatedParameters__ControlPoints.txt / ...Momenta.txt), plus <deformation-parameters> (kernel-width, kernel-type torch, number--timepoints). Output Shooting__GeodesicFlow__<obj>__tp_<>__age_<t>.vtk; take max-tp file. Kernel/device: <kernel-type>torch</kernel-type> + <kernel-device>auto|cpu|cuda</kernel-device> model (use-cuda optimization_parameters.xml — Deformetrica 4.3 warns “Unknown entry” key ignores ). Momenta.txt leading n_subjects n_cp dim header row ControlPoints.txt — Deformetrica writes reads , external parser must account . Object types Landmark, PolyLine, SurfaceMesh (old PointCloud/OrientedPolyLine/…). Attachment types: Landmark (ordered L2), Varifold, Current. R/deformetrica4.R (new, branch) wraps path: find_deformetrica() + deformetrica_shoot(x, control_points, momenta, kernel_width, ...).

what: R Client for the Deformetrica Shape Registration Toolkit
title: R Client for the Deformetrica Shape Registration Toolkit
text: deformetricar R client Deformetrica shape-registration toolkit (>= 4.3). lets fit diffeomorphisms 3D shapes — point clouds, neuron backbones surface meshes — apply (geodesic shooting) arbitrary objects, sensible defaults registering fly connectome neurons. also reads writes VTK object formats. Deformetrica 4 replaced old ShootAndFlow3 C++ binaries single deformetrica CLI (estimate / compute); deformetricar wraps modern interface.  Left: whole Aedes aegypti brain surface warped onto Drosophila JRC2018F template. Right: just central complex — mosquito’s CBU/CBL onto fly’s fan-shaped ellipsoid bodies. produced end--end mosquito--fly vignette.

Work µm-scale coordinates (∼O(1–100)); fit nm can silently collapse identity warp. useful knobs, least impactful: Rules thumb: give strong homologous objects small data_sigma hull larger one; small structure lags, reduce kernel_width (global) cranking weight; keep object_kernel_width ≥ target mesh’s face spacing. data_sigma object_kernel_width per object, can tune specific pairings (e.g. central-complex objects) independently rest.

Names become object ids. kernel_width Deformation kernel width. object_kernel_width Data-attachment kernel width — spatial scale object's surface/curve mismatch measured (Current/Varifold). Smaller = finer, local matching, pulls small structures (galls, noduli) onto target tightly. single value every object, per-object vector (recycled length 1; matched name sources named, else order). Smaller sigma weights object strongly fit — e.g. give homologous neuropils small sigma outer hull larger one neuropils drive internal alignment. landmark_sigma Data-attachment sigma optional shared landmarks object (defaults mean data_sigma). timepoints, max_iterations, device, deformetrica, workdir, verbose deformetrica_register().

title: deformetricar 1.0.0
text: major modernisation: deformetricar now focused client Deformetrica (>= 4.3), meeting current R package standards. 1.0.0 release marks clean break 10-year-old, L1-larva-specific ShootAndFlow3/sparseMatching3 era: package now template- species-agnostic. old L1 symmetrisation helpers (symmetrisel1(), otherside(), apply.mirror.affine()) longer exported — rebuilt scratch walkthrough L1 vignette, showing compose symmetrising warp general API.

title: deformetricar 1.0.0
text: deformetrica_register() deformetrica_shoot(): fit apply diffeomorphisms via Deformetrica 4 estimate / compute CLI. deformetrica_register_multi(): fit ONE diffeomorphism whole set matched objects (flyconnectome/deformetricaLR recipe), optional shared landmark regulariser. accepts per-object data_sigma per-object object_kernel_width (scalar, named positional vectors) object can weighted matched scale one fit — e.g. strong homologous neuropils weak outer hull. deformetrica_shoot() guards degenerate single-control-point case (made Deformetrica’s torch kernel die cryptic IndexError) clear message. deformetrica_shoot(flow = TRUE) returns every timepoint geodesic flow, animating warp. Object-aware /O: registration shooting accept point matrices, mesh3d surfaces (SurfaceMesh + Current, faces preserved) nat neurons (PolyLine, via new write_neuron_vtk()); shooting returns class given. affine_prealign(): centre + isotropic-scale + ICP pre-alignment returning apply() closure replays identical transform objects. split_mesh_lr() mirror_lr_split(): split surface left/right halves — midline plane, (even unpaired midline object) self-mirror registration labels point travel toward mirror partner. ggplot_flow_gif(): animate geodesic flow GIF, object coloured reference volume. takes volume_col (draw brain hull soft coloured envelope, e.g. light pink), set static targets drawn beneath flow transparent greyscale ramp (coloured moving structures can read landing fixed targets match), rotation_matrix view. general animation machinery now lives nat.ggplot::ggneuron_gif() (>= 1.1.0); ggplot_flow_gif() thin convenience wrapper around , assembler falls back magick gifski (needs Rust toolchain) unavailable. install_deformetrica(): one-call setup Deformetrica (>= 4.3) CLI reticulate-managed conda env / virtualenv; find_deformetrica() resolves automatically. transform_vtk() (renamed unexported transform.vtk, R mistook S3 method) now exported. find_deformetrica() locates executable PATH, via options(deformetricar.exe=), install_deformetrica() environment, conda environment. Vignettes, GIFs: mosquito--fly (Aedes → Drosophila JRC2018F, whole-brain + central complex), fafb-left-right (FAFB brain mirror registration), l1-symmetrise-cognates (symmetrise L1 larval CNS VFB CATMAID find left-right cognates).

title: deformetricar 1.0.0
text: read.vtk() / write.vtk() renamed read_vtk() / write_vtk() longer shadow nat::write.vtk() (S3 generic). nat’s neuron VTK writes multi-point polylines, whereas Deformetrica 4 needs 2-point line segments, two share method — hence distinct names. Removed defunct Deformetrica 2.1 shootflow() / ShootAndFlow3 path — use deformetrica_shoot(). Removed L1 larval mirroring / cognate helpers (symmetrisel1(), apply.mirror.affine(), otherside(), findcognate() friends) bundled transforms. scientific workflow now lives l1-symmetrise-cognates vignette, driven live VFB dataset.
